chunks.text: Ptolemy II Installation Ptolemy II Installation See the Ptolemy II 3.0 release notes for details about this release. The instructions on this page concern installing rebuilding Ptolemy II from sources. Instructions for the impatient Install JDK1.4.0

s. Instructions for the impatient Install JDK1.4.0 or later, add the jdk bin directory to your path so that javac and java are available, and have the make command available. Set the PTII environment variable to the top level Ptolemy directory, that

variable to the top level Ptolemy directory, that is the directory above this directory bash PTII=c:\\Program\ Files\\Ptolemy\\Ptolemy\ II\ 2.0 export PTII csh setenv PTII /users/yourname/ptII Run configure cd "$PTII" rm -f config.* ./configure Ptol

nfigure cd "$PTII" rm -f config.* ./configure Ptolemy is shipped with .class and .jar files, but the $PTII/bin/scripts needs to be updated. The safest thing to do is to run make fast install on the entire tree: make fast install Start vergil $PTII/

entire tree: make fast install Start vergil $PTII/bin/vergil Requirements The PTII variable The PTII environment variable is used to refer to the directory that contains the Ptolemy II tree. Setting PTII is covered in the Unix and Windows sections be

TII is covered in the Unix and Windows sections below. Java Ptolemy II has been developed under JDK1.4.1_02, which is available from http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.4/ The Java download is available in several formats. JRE - Java Runtime Environment Runti

eral formats. JRE - Java Runtime Environment Runtime only If you are planning on only using the prebuilt Ptolemy II actors, you can download the JRE. JDK - Java Development Kit Runtime and development tools like the Java compiler javac If you are pla

tools like the Java compiler javac If you are planning on writing your own actors or recompiling from sources, download the JDK. If you install the JDK, be sure that the bin directory is in your path so that the javac and java commands are availabl

h so that the javac and java commands are available. Java Plug-in The applet tags have been configured to require JDK1.4, so to run the applets in Mozilla 1.x, Netscape 4.7 or Internet Explorer 6.0 under Windows, you will need to use the Java Plug-in

der Windows, you will need to use the Java Plug-in from http://java.sun.com/products/plugin/ JavaSoft . Currently, the Java Plug-in 1.4 is included in the JDK1.4 download for Windows. If you are running under Solaris, you can download a separate plug

ng under Solaris, you can download a separate plug-in or a complete Netscape installation. If your platform is not supported by the plug-in, you can run the applets with the JDK appletviewer command. See the Plug-in section of the Ptolemy II Troubles

See the Plug-in section of the Ptolemy II Troubleshooting guide for details. Optional packages that are shipped with Ptolemy Diva Diva is a visualization package used by some of the demos and by vergil, the Ptolemy II user interface. The Ptolemy II d

l, the Ptolemy II user interface. The Ptolemy II distribution include $PTII/lib/diva.jar . The Diva sources and documentation are available at http://www.gigascale.org/diva . Jython Jython is a Java implementation of a subset of Python. The PythonScr

mplementation of a subset of Python. The PythonScript actor requires Jython. Usually, one not need install Jython as all Ptolemy II distributions that include the PythonScript actor also include Jython as $PTII/lib/jython.jar . The Ptolemy II source

on as $PTII/lib/jython.jar . The Ptolemy II source distribution also includes $PTII/lib/jython.jar . Jython is covered by a Jython specific copyright If, for some reason you want to install Jython by hand: Download Jython from http://www.jython.org F

hand: Download Jython from http://www.jython.org Follow the instructions in http://www.jython.org/install.html Basically, you should do something like java -classpath . jython-21 and then install Jython in $PTII/vendors/jython Configure looks for $PT

on in $PTII/vendors/jython Configure looks for $PTII/lib/jython.jar and then $PTII/vendors/jython/jython.jar Jacl Jacl is a 100% Java implementation of Tcl that we use for testing Ptolemy II. Each Java directory contains a test subdirectory that con

va directory contains a test subdirectory that contains Tcl files that use Jacl to test the Ptolemy II Java code. Jacl is only necessary if you are planning on running the Ptolemy II test suite. We ship a customized version of Jacl called Ptjacl. The

ip a customized version of Jacl called Ptjacl. The primary difference between Ptjacl and Jacl1.1 is that Ptjacl is shipped as one jar file. The Ptjacl jar file at $PTII/lib/ptjacl.jar is shipped with Ptolemy II. For more information about Jacl and Tc

Ptolemy II. For more information about Jacl and Tcl Blend, see Activestate.com Jacl and Tcl Blend page . Christopher Hylands' Java Page. java:: man page Saxon The CalInterpreter Actor requires Saxon. The Saxon XSLT Processor from Michael Kay can be

The Saxon XSLT Processor from Michael Kay can be found at http://saxon.sourceforge.net/ Note that we modified saxon.jar so that it does not include the jar file entry META-INF/services/javax.xml.transform.TransformerFactory net.sf.saxon.TransformerF

sform.TransformerFactory net.sf.saxon.TransformerFactoryImpl If this file is present in saxon7.jar , then the HSIF tool will not work. Soot Soot is used by the code generator . Soot is a Java optimization framework from http://www.sable.mcgill.ca/so

ation framework from http://www.sable.mcgill.ca/soot/ If the code generator is present, then we include sootclasses.jar and jasminclasses.jar in $PTII/lib Soot and Jasmin are both covered by the Soot and Jasmin specific copyright Optional packages no

and Jasmin specific copyright Optional packages not shipped with Ptolemy used at runtime The packages below are used at runtime. Parts of the prebuilt Ptolemy II installers use these packages to provide added functionality. For size or licensing re

ide added functionality. For size or licensing reasons, we do not include these packages with the prebuilt Ptolemy releases, so you may want to install these packages if you are interested in using the functionality they provide. JAI The Java Advanc

he functionality they provide. JAI The Java Advanced Imaging (JAI) actors require that JAI, which can be downloaded from http://java.sun.com/products/java-media/jai/ You need not install JAI if you are not planning on using the JAI Actors. We verifi

re not planning on using the JAI Actors. We verified that JAI 1.1.2-rc works with these actors. Note that under Web Start, we had to install the JAI jar files by hand in c:/Program Files/java/j2re1.4.1_02/lib/ext by copying jai_codec.jar , jai_core.j

1_02/lib/ext by copying jai_codec.jar , jai_core.jar , and mlibwrapper_jai.jar from the c:/j2sdk1.4.1_02/jre/lib/ext Java3d The 3-D graphics (GR) domain demos require Java3d, which can be installed from http://java.sun.com/products/java-media/3D/ .

om http://java.sun.com/products/java-media/3D/ . You need not install Java3d if you are not planning on using GR domain. If you plan on recompiling the GR domain, then install the Java 3D SDK and runtime. If you plan on only running the GR domain d

time. If you plan on only running the GR domain demos, then installing only the Java 3D runtime should be sufficient. We have verified the demos with the Java 3D 1.2.1_04 SDK (DirectX Version). Note that JDK1.4.0 requires Java 3D 1.2.1_04. If you r

that JDK1.4.0 requires Java 3D 1.2.1_04. If you run with Java 3D 1.2.1_03 and JDK1.4.0, the GR demos will fail to initialize and you will see the message: Fail to create back buffer - DDERR_INALIDPARAM The Java 3D 1.2.1_04 release notes say that thi

AM The Java 3D 1.2.1_04 release notes say that this bug: 4470749 Java3D fail to initialize underlying OGL/D3D library in jdk1.4 was fixed in Java 3D 1.2.1_04 Joystick The Joystick actor requires the Joystick interface from http://sourceforge.net/proj

oystick interface from http://sourceforge.net/projects/javajoystick/ . The Joystick interface works under Windows and Linux. Joystick is covered by a Joystick specific copyright You must download and install the Joystick interface to use the Joystick

install the Joystick interface to use the Joystick actor. Configure looks for the joystick interface in $PTII/vendors/misc/joystick . In that directory, it looks for joystick.jar . Download the Joystick 0.6 zip file from http://sourceforge.net/proj

tick 0.6 zip file from http://sourceforge.net/projects/javajoystick/ Unzip it in $PTII/vendors/misc Under Windows, copy the .dll into your path cp $PTII/vendors/misc/joystick/lib/jjstick.dll $PTII/bin JMF The Java Media Framework (JMF) actors require

JMF The Java Media Framework (JMF) actors require that JMF, which can be downloaded from http://java.sun.com/products/java-media/jmf/ You need not install JMF if you are not planning on using the JMF Actors. We verified that JMF 2.1.1c works with t

F Actors. We verified that JMF 2.1.1c works with these actors. Matlab Ptolemy II includes an interface to Matlab that was developed by Zoltan Kemenczy and Sean Simmons of Research in Motion Limited . The Matlab interface works only under Windows an

. The Matlab interface works only under Windows and it requires that Matlab be installed on the local machine. The Matlab interface has been tested with Matlab 6.1, which corresponds with R12.1. The Matlab Expression actor can access Matlab methods

Matlab Expression actor can access Matlab methods to process matrices and plot data using the Matlab plotter. A demo can be found at $PTII/ptolemy/matlab/demo/MatlabExpression/MatlabExpression.xml Compiling the Matlab interface The Matlab interface w

piling the Matlab interface The Matlab interface works under Windows and Linux. In theory, the Matlab interface might work under Solaris, but we have not tested it. If you do not have gcc or dlltool installed, you can install them as part of the Cygw

nstalled, you can install them as part of the Cygwin installation The Matlab interface is implemented using the Java Native Interface (JNI). When $PTII/configure is run, it look in the user path for a binary called matlab , determines the location o

a binary called matlab , determines the location of the Matlab extern/include/libeng.dif file and then checks for gcc and dlltool . If these components were not found at when configure was run, then you can rerun configure, remake and run the demo wi

ou can rerun configure, remake and run the demo with: cd $PTII rm config.*; ./configure cd $PTII/ptolemy/matlab make $PTII/bin/vergil ./demo/MatlabExpression/MatlabExpression Optional Packages not shipped with Ptolemy that are used at compile time Th

pped with Ptolemy that are used at compile time The packages below are used when Ptolemy II is compiled. You need not install these packages if you are running a prebuilt version of Ptolemy II. If you install a package after building Ptolemy II, reru

install a package after building Ptolemy II, rerun configure cd $PTII; rm config.*; ./configure and then make make fast make Ptolemy II uses makefiles to build. We include a subset of the Cygwin tools for Windows, see the Cygwin installation notes.

ls for Windows, see the Cygwin installation notes. Ptolemy II has been built with GNU make 3.77, but does not use GNU make extensions, so GNU make is not requires. Ptolemy II has also been built with Solaris 8 /usr/ccs/bin/make . It seems that the m

Solaris 8 /usr/ccs/bin/make . It seems that the make that is included with SGI Irix 6.5 may have problems, the workaround is to installed GNU make. GC The Copernicus C code generator uses the Boehm Garbarge Collector, which can be found at http://ww

arbarge Collector, which can be found at http://www.hpl.hp.com/personal/Hans_Boehm/gc/ You need not download the Garbage Collector unless you are planning on using the Copernicus C code generator. The configure program looks for the Garbage Collector

configure program looks for the Garbage Collector in $PTII/vendors/gc . To install the Garbage Collector: Download from http://www.hpl.hp.com/personal/Hans_Boehm/gc/gc_source/ cd $PTII/vendors/gc tar -zxf /tmp/gc.tar.gz cd gc6.1 ./configure --prefix

-zxf /tmp/gc.tar.gz cd gc6.1 ./configure --prefix=$PTII make make check make install Note that under Windows, PTII cannot have a backslash in it or else make will have problems. As of 4/03, we were using version 6.1 CaffeineMark The CaffeineMark ben

sing version 6.1 CaffeineMark The CaffeineMark benchmark is used by the Copernicus C code generator. You need not download the CaffeineMark benchmark unless you are interested in benchmarking the Copernicus C code generator. The main page for the Ca

ernicus C code generator. The main page for the CaffeineMark is http://www-sor.inria.fr/~java/tools/cmkit To install, do cd $PTII/vendors/cm # Download http://www-sor.inria.fr/~java/tools/cmkit/embed.zip jar -xf embed.zip JavaCC JavaCC is the Java Co

zip jar -xf embed.zip JavaCC JavaCC is the Java Compiler Compiler, which similar to yacc and lex . JavaCC is only necessary if you modify the expression parser in ptolemy/data/expr . Download javacc-3.0.tar.gz from http://www.experimentalstuff.com/Te

.0.tar.gz from http://www.experimentalstuff.com/Technologies/JavaCC/ Create $PTII/vendors/sun if necessary mkdir -p $PTII/vendors/sun Extract cd $PTII/vendors/sun tar -zxf where_ever_you_downloaded /javacc-3.0.tar.gz Re run configure: cd $PTII; rm co

avacc-3.0.tar.gz Re run configure: cd $PTII; rm config.*; ./configure Rebuilding The Ptolemy II distribution includes the compiled Java .class files and the javadoc output, so it is not necessary to 'build' or run make to use Ptolemy II. Unix If you

build' or run make to use Ptolemy II. Unix If you would like to rebuild from the Java sources under Unix, you should set the PTII environment variable in your environment. Under C Shell: setenv PTII /users/ptII You may want to add that line to your

/users/ptII You may want to add that line to your ~/.cshrc file. To configure the makefiles, run $PTII/configure , which is a GNU autoconf script that looks for your Java compiler, reads in $PTII/mk/ptII.mk.in and generates $PTII/mk/ptII.mk : cd "$PT

tII.mk.in and generates $PTII/mk/ptII.mk : cd "$PTII" rm -f config.* ./configure $PTII/configure creates $PTII/config.status and $PTII/config.cache . If you would like to reconfigure Ptolemy II to use a different Java compiler, set your path so that

e a different Java compiler, set your path so that the appropriate compiler is first in your path, then remove config.status and rerun configure. You may also use the --with-java= configure command line argument. For more information, run configure

ine argument. For more information, run configure -help . To build: make fast all Windows Ptolemy II was developed under Windows XP. We have successfully installed Ptolemy II under Windows '98, but if you have problems under Windows '98 we recomme

if you have problems under Windows '98 we recommend upgrading Windows XP. To build Ptolemy II under Windows XP: Install JDK 1.4 from http://java.sun.com/j2se/1.4/ Be sure to add the bin directory to your path so that javac and java are available. In

your path so that javac and java are available. Install the Cygwin toolkit from http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/ See the Ptolemy II 3.0 Website for Cygwin downloads that include the tools necessary to build Ptolemy II. An alternative to using the Cy

o build Ptolemy II. An alternative to using the Cygwin downloads we provide is to download directly from the Cygwin website. Complete installation instructions can be found at http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/faq , The Cygwin installer tends to chang

m/cygwin/faq , The Cygwin installer tends to change over time, but below is a summary of the steps necessary: Create an empty directory Download http://sources.redhat.com/cygwin/setup.exe and save it in the empty directory Click on setup , then Next

in the empty directory Click on setup , then Next Select Install from Internet , then click on Next Use the default install root directory, which should be c:/cygwin Select 'Default Text File Type' of DOS. If you select Unix instead of DOS, then you

f DOS. If you select Unix instead of DOS, then you will likely have Cygwin CR/NL problems with CVS. Then hit the Next button. Use the default Local Package Directory, which should be the directory you created above. If you are not behind a firewall,

u created above. If you are not behind a firewall, click on direct connection, then click Next Select a nearby mirror, then click Next You will be presented with a list of packages to install. The initial default installs packages from Base , which

tial default installs packages from Base , which is roughly a 6Mb download that expands into about 26Mb of files. If you are serious about development you may also want to install the packages below. To install an individual package, cl

ges below. To install an individual package, click on the + to the left of the group name, then click on the arrows to the right of the group name until Default changes to Install Devel Category autoconf - requires gawk and m4 from the

Category autoconf - requires gawk and m4 from the Interpreters Category binutils, gcc, gettext, libintl libintl1, libtool, libtool-devel - The matlab interface requires gcc and Matlab. You may find it easier to install all of the Devel catego

nd it easier to install all of the Devel category cvs Net Category openssh - cvs may need this openssl - required by openssh Text Category: less Hit Next , which should download the packages and install them Click on Add icon to Start Menu , and t

stall them Click on Add icon to Start Menu , and then Finish . Start up bash with Start -> Cygnus Solutions -> Cygwin Bash Shell Set the value of the PTII environment variable to the name of the directory where Ptolemy II is installed. Under Windows

tory where Ptolemy II is installed. Under Windows XP, you set the environment variable via the Environment tab of the System control panel ( Start Menu -> Settings -> Control Panels -> System -> Advanced -> Environment Variables ) The PTII value shou

ced -> Environment Variables ) The PTII value should be set using back slashes so that javac gets the right path if you've installed Cygwin in a location other than C:\ such as C:\Cygwin . If you have installed Cygwin in c:/cygwin, and your Ptolemy I

installed Cygwin in c:/cygwin, and your Ptolemy II installation is in a directory outside of the c:/cygwin tree, then Cygwin may change your PTII variable to include "/cygdrive/c". This will cause problems with non-Cygwin tools like javac that do n

roblems with non-Cygwin tools like javac that do not understand /cygdrive/c. The best fix for this is to move your home directory outside of c:/cygwin. An alternative is to place your Ptolemy II tree outside of c:/cygwin. How to tell Cygwin where yo

outside of c:/cygwin. How to tell Cygwin where your home directory is Cygwin gets the location of your home directory from a line that contains your login in c:/cygwin/etc/passwd . If your login is not listed, then run mkpasswd -l To list all the

not listed, then run mkpasswd -l To list all the local accounts and add the line for your account to c:/cygwin/etc/passwd using workdpad . To start wordpad , do Start -> Run and type in wordpad . Note: you can use mkpasswd -help to get help. If you

e: you can use mkpasswd -help to get help. If you are logged in to the domain and not the local machine, try mkpasswd -d | grep yourlogin to get just your login Edit c:/cygwin/etc/passwd with wordpad and change the home directory from /home/ yourlogi

and change the home directory from /home/ yourlogin to /cygdrive/c/users/ yourlogin , which will set your home directory as c:/users/ yourlogin Save c:/cyginw/etc/passwd Start up a new bash shell and see where your home directory is by typing pwd An

see where your home directory is by typing pwd An example PTII value would be: c:\users\yourname\ptII Cygwin directory mapping Note that Cygwin remaps directories, so d:\tmp may appear as /cygdrive/d/tmp . Unfortunately, the java command does not und

tmp . Unfortunately, the java command does not understand the Cygwin /cygdrive notation, so java may not find the files it needs. For example, pwd returns /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII , which will not work with java : cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ PTI

th java : cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ PTII=d:\\tmp\\ptII cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ echo $PTII d:\tmp\ptII cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ cd $PTII cxh@MOWAT-171 /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII $ pwd /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII So, under Cyg

/tmp/ptII $ pwd /cygdrive/d/tmp/ptII So, under Cygwin, it is best to avoid using pwd when setting variables. Avoid: PTII=`pwd`/ptII and use PTII=d:\\tmp\\ptII instead. Note that the configure script checks for this sort of thing. In the Environment

checks for this sort of thing. In the Environment tab, you should also set your path to include the location of the JDK bin directory (i.e. C:\j2sdk1.4.1_01\bin ) When you run cygwin.bat, your path should be set to include the Cygwin tools. You can

hould be set to include the Cygwin tools. You can test this by typing type make , which should return make is /usr/bin/make If make cannot be found, then you may also need to add the Cygwin bin directory c:\cygwin\usr\bin to your path. Optional: Ins

tory c:\cygwin\usr\bin to your path. Optional: Install JavaCC, see the instructions above . Start up Cygwin bash , and cd to the Ptolemy II directory. Run rm -f config.* Run ./configure Run make fast all Now type vergil (or $PTII/bin/vergil if you di

all Now type vergil (or $PTII/bin/vergil if you did not set your path) in the C shell or cygwin Bash window and the Ptolemy II GUI should start up Currently, Ptolemy II does not support building via Microsoft Visual Studio nmake under Windows. Also,

icrosoft Visual Studio nmake under Windows. Also, we have not tried building Ptolemy II under Windows '95 or Windows '98. The instructions for setting up a parallel tree using CVS can be found in $PTII/doc/coding/develsetup . Last Updated: $Date: 20
II/doc/coding/develsetup . Last Updated: $Date: 2003/05/13 15:04:45 $
